chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 6181 of 2024 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge(s) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For hearing of main case. 23.12.2024 Mr. Ramiz Naseem, advocate for the petitioner. Ms. Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General. Ms. Rafia Altaf, Sub-Inspector, FIA, I&AHS, JIAP. DSP Ayaz Ahmed, Foreign Registration Office, Special Branch, Karachi. Comments filed on behalf of respondent department are taken on record. The relevant constituents whereof reads as follows: “COMMENTS ON BEHALF OF RESPONDENT NO.03 i.e. DIRECTOR, FIA SINDH and RESPONDENT NO.05 i.e. ADDITIONAL DEPUTY DIRECTOR, FIA I&AHS JIAP, KARACHI Respectfully Sheweth, It is respectfully prayed that petitioner MD Ismail & other have filed the Constitutional Petition No. D-6181 of 2024 before this Hon'ble Court which is fixed on 23.12.2024. Hence, comments on the subject petition on the above named petitioner are submitted as under PARA-WISE COMMENTS: 1. Para No.1 to 4:- No comments, as the same does not relate to the answering respondent no. 03 & 05. 2. Para No.5 to 7:- Para No.4 to 07 are vehemently denied being false and fabricated. 3. Para No.8 to 16:- That the contents of Para No.08 to 16 needs no comments as the same does not relate to the answering respondent no.03 & 05. It is respectfully submitted that previously, the Deportees, who produced lawful requisite documents at FIA, Immigration Counter Departure, Karachi have already travelled to Bangladesh. Further submitted that, there is no delay on the part of the answering Respondents for the travelling abroad / deportation of the petitioners.

It is respectfully submitted that previously, the Deportees, who produced lawful requisite documents at FIA, Immigration Counter Departure, Karachi have already travelled to Bangladesh. Further submitted that, there is no delay on the part of the answering Respondents for the travelling abroad / deportation of the petitioners. However, it is pertinent to mention here that the following documents are required at the time of departure on deportation i.e. (i) travel permit(s) issued from the High Commission of Bangladesh, (ii) Order from the concerned Home Department, (iii) letter from Foreign Registration Office, Special Branch, Karachi having the request for their deportation, (iv) travel permit issued by Special Branch and (v) a letter issued from Additional Inspector General Police to Foreign Registration Office. In view of the above, whenever any of the Petitioner will appear at FIA Immigration Counter along with lawful requisite documents, the individual(s) will be allowed to proceed abroad. The answering Respondent No.03 & 04 shall abide any order / directives, passed by this Hon'ble Court in this regard.” Learned counsel for the petitioners’ states that requirements as stated aforesaid shall be complied with and thereafter the petitioner be permitted to travel. Ms. Rafia Altaf, Sub-Inspector, FIA, states that the department has no cavil to the aforesaid as already stated in the comments. It is jointly stated that the petition be disposed of as aforesaid. Order accordingly. Judge Judge Khuhro/PA
